evals.prompt: Posts aren't publishing on our Postiz instance. Walk me through a complete troubleshooting checklist — what are the most common causes and how do I fix each one?
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive troubleshooting covering orchestrator, Temporal, subscription, image format, and container health.

assertions.name: orchestrator-check
assertions.description: Mentions checking if orchestrator is running in docker compose command

assertions.name: temporal-check
assertions.description: Mentions checking Temporal container health

assertions.name: subscription-check
assertions.description: Mentions checking Subscription row in database

assertions.name: post-status-check
assertions.description: Mentions checking post status is QUEUE not DRAFT

assertions.name: container-health
assertions.description: Mentions checking all 4 containers are running

assertions.name: docker-logs-command
assertions.description: Provides docker logs command for inspection

assertions.name: restart-procedure
assertions.description: Shows restart procedure (docker compose down/up)

assertions.name: image-format-gotcha
assertions.description: Mentions image format issue ([{"id":"uuid"}] vs ["uuid"])

assertions.name: missing-post-recovery
assertions.description: Mentions missing-post-workflow or changing status back to QUEUE for recovery

assertions.name: no-temporal-db-direct
assertions.description: Warns against directly modifying Temporal database

evals.prompt: I want to create and schedule a new social media post programmatically via the Postiz API. Show me the complete flow including image upload and scheduling across Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn.
evals.expected_output: Complete API flow: upload media, create post with correct image format, schedule across channels.

assertions.name: api-endpoint
assertions.description: References Postiz API endpoint (/api/posts or social.freyaivisions.de)

assertions.name: media-upload
assertions.description: Shows media upload step before post creation

assertions.name: image-format-correct
assertions.description: Uses correct image format: [{"id": ...}] object array

assertions.name: publish-date
assertions.description: Includes publishDate or schedule time in post creation

assertions.name: channels-array
assertions.description: Includes channels array for multi-platform posting

assertions.name: organization-id
assertions.description: Includes organizationId in the request

assertions.name: auth-header
assertions.description: Includes Authorization header or API key

assertions.name: three-platforms
assertions.description: Mentions all 3 platforms (Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn)

assertions.name: queue-status
assertions.description: Mentions post will be in QUEUE status awaiting Temporal

assertions.name: no-wrong-image-format
assertions.description: Does NOT use the wrong string array image format

evals.prompt: After updating Postiz containers, the app shows 'Subscription required'. What happened and how do I fix it? Also, what other things should I check after a container update?
evals.expected_output: Explains subscription row loss, provides fix SQL, and post-update checklist.

assertions.name: subscription-row-lost
assertions.description: Explains that updates can wipe the Subscription row

assertions.name: insert-sql
assertions.description: Provides INSERT SQL for Subscription table

assertions.name: org-id-correct
assertions.description: Uses correct organization ID (c740c0dd-...)

assertions.name: docker-exec-command
assertions.description: Shows docker exec command to run SQL

assertions.name: orchestrator-verify
assertions.description: Mentions verifying orchestrator is still in docker compose command after update

assertions.name: channel-reauth
assertions.description: Mentions checking if channels need re-authentication

assertions.name: all-containers-check
assertions.description: Mentions verifying all 4 containers are running

assertions.name: temporal-health
assertions.description: Mentions checking Temporal health after update

assertions.name: no-temporal-db-hack
assertions.description: Does NOT suggest modifying Temporal database directly
